Q15 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Prying forces are the additional

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Prying forces are additional tensile stresses that develop in bolts when the connected components
are not perfectly rigid and flex under load, causing the bolts to experience increased tension
beyond the initial preload.

Q24 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Equilibrium condition, yield condition (M < MP) and mechanism condition (formation of a plastic collapse mechanism) are the conditions to be satisfied by any correct plastic analysis result. Which of the above conditions does the kinematic method of plastic analysis consider?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The kinematic theorem satisfies the equilibrium and mechanism (continuity) conditions.

Q28 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding lacing bars used in a built-up column. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, d)
The maximum spacing of lacing bars ‘C‘ should be such that the minimum slenderness ratio of the
component member is not greater than 50 or 0.7 times the slenderness ratio of the member as a
whole.
